chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI High Court Appeal No. 98 of 2019 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of main case. 2. For hearing of CMA No. 2962/2018. 01.12.2025 Mr. Tasleem, advocate holding brief for Ms. Sofia Saeed, advocate for appellant. Mr. Ghazi Khan Khalil, advocate along with Mr. Ameer Nausherwan Adil, Zeeshan Ahmed, Kumail Abbas and Aftab Ali, advocates for Respondent. Ms. Zehrah Sehr Vayani, Assistant Attorney General. Mr. Raja Love Kush, Deputy Manager (Legal), SSGC. Mr. Ali Haider, advocate holding brief for Mr. Kashif Hanif, Advocate for respondent OGRA. This high court appeal challenges an interim order dated 23.01.2019 in Suit No. 47/2019, which reads as follows:- “This is a matter in respect of Sunday closure of gas supply. A learned Single Judge of this Court vide Order dated 12.10.2018 passed in Suit No.1149/2017 alongwith other connected matters, has been pleased to dismiss the injunction applications, against which though High Court Appeals have been preferred; however, neither the judgment has been suspended nor set-aside and the order is only to the extent of no coercive action, which is applicable inter se parties. In view of such position, the injunction application is dismissed for the reasons recorded in Order dated 12.10.2018 passed in Suit No.1149/2017 alongwith other connected matters.” Learned counsel for the appellant remains unable to assist and seeks time. This appeal is clogging the docket for more than six years and it appears that no justifiable reason exists to perpetuate the same.

In view of such position, the injunction application is dismissed for the reasons recorded in Order dated 12.10.2018 passed in Suit No.1149/2017 alongwith other connected matters.” Learned counsel for the appellant remains unable to assist and seeks time. This appeal is clogging the docket for more than six years and it appears that no justifiable reason exists to perpetuate the same. This high court appeal is dismissed for non-prosecution. Judge Judge Ayaz P.S.
